title: Former Bruins enforcer, radio host Lyndon Byers diagnosed with CTE after his death
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lyndon Byers, former Boston Bruins player, was diagnosed with Stage 3 CTE after his death at 61.
Byers donated his brain to Boston University's CTE Center to help raise awareness about the disease's effects.
Byers' family disclosed the diagnosis to highlight CTE risks in contact sports like hockey and football.

title: Drake Maye gives a vote of confidence for Will Campbell after playoff struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye supports Will Campbell despite his struggles during the Patriots' Super Bowl LX loss to Seattle.
Rookie Will Campbell faced increased pressure after returning from a torn MCL, allowing 14 pressures in the Super Bowl.
Patriots drafted tackle Caleb Lomu as a potential backup plan if Campbell's performance issues persist next season.

title: 2 women attempt to swim from Marblehead to Scituate to qualify for Ireland marathon swims
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Abigail Fairman and Sarah Roberts attempted an 18-mile swim from Marblehead to Scituate but had to stop due to harsh conditions.
The swim qualified them for Ireland marathon swims, requiring 6-8 hours in 55-57°F water, in July and August.
Both women are experienced marathon swimmers; Roberts has completed 334 Alcatraz swims, while Fairman has swum in Tasmania.

title: Preservation efforts ramp up as Swansea Friends Meeting House is named among nation’s most endangered historic sites
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Swansea Friends Meeting House in Somerset is among the 11 most endangered historic sites in the U.S.
The Friends of Somerset Historic Preservation secured an $184,300 grant for restoration and site improvements.
Plans include transforming the Meeting House into a community cultural center after restoration efforts are complete.

title: Cape Cod’s Sundae School to offer 76-cent ice cream scoops
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sundae School in Cape Cod celebrates 50 years with 76-cent ice cream scoops on June 14.
The anniversary deal applies from 2 to 4 p.m. at Harwich Port and Dennisport locations.
The 76-cent price honors the shop's opening year, 1976, acknowledging its legacy in Massachusetts.

title: July 4th fireworks cancellation sparks intense pushback in Rutland
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rutland officials canceled the July 4th festivities due to staffing limitations after a tax override rejection.
Residents demand refunds for donations, but officials cite expenses and future event funding as reasons to withhold them.
The Massachusetts Fiscal Alliance criticized the cancellation and called for an investigation into the fund management.

title: 2 teen girls arrested after Boston police allegedly find multiple firearms, drugs, and a knife in vehicle
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two teenage girls arrested in Dorchester after police find firearms, drugs, and a knife in their vehicle.
A 16-year-old from Taunton and 18-year-old Jacqueline Rose Botelho face multiple charges, including possession without a license.
Botelho, held at Suffolk County Jail, pleaded not guilty; her next court appearance is on June 22.

title: T pushing new PSA after fatal escalator incident
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The MBTA plans a campaign on escalator emergency stop buttons after a fatal incident at Davis Station in February.
Steven McCluskey died after being trapped in an escalator; his clothing got caught, and help arrived 20 minutes later.
MBTA General Manager Phil Eng emphasized safety improvements and potential upgrades for surveillance methods across the transit system.

title: A 3BR cabin-style home near the Berkshires — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A three-bedroom cabin-style home in Charlemont spans 2,185 square feet and includes 14.6 acres.
Built in 1977, the property features three full baths and one half bath and private sewer and water.
The home's taxes are $6,396 for 2025; the listing price options are $825K, $625K, or $425K.

title: Mass. photographer stabbed in ‘unprovoked attack’ while shooting wedding in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Donald Halsing, 26, a photographer from Mendon, Mass., was stabbed at a wedding reception in Raymond, Maine.
Halsing underwent emergency surgery for serious injuries, including liver laceration, but is expected to recover.
Andrew Manderson, a guest at the wedding, was arrested and charged with elevated aggravated assault.
